briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of embedded banking middleware provider Synapse, raising serious questions about consumer protections in fintech.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving a new wave of unconventional credit card products that challenge traditional banking models.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking startup Credit Kudos signals big tech's growing ambitions in financial data and lending.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Are Canadians reaching their 'breaking point'? New data shows more people filing for insolvency](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/insolvency-filings-increasing-canada-9.7197230?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Rising insolvency filings across Canada signal growing financial stress among consumers, with implications for credit unions, lenders, and financial inclusion advocates.

- **[Canada wants sovereign AI data centres. What does that actually mean?](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bakx-canada-sovereign-ai-9.7198649?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada is pushing to build sovereign AI data infrastructure, a move with significant implications for domestic fintech companies relying on data privacy and cloud services.

- **[Goldman Sachs to acquire Canadian data centre platform QScale](https://betakit.com/goldman-sachs-to-acquire-canadian-data-centre-platform-qscale/)** — *BetaKit* — Goldman Sachs is acquiring Quebec-based data centre platform QScale, highlighting growing global financial interest in Canadian digital infrastructure.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's push to replace screen scraping with secure open banking APIs remains a foundational policy milestone shaping the country's evolving financial data framework.

- **[Clio tops $500 million USD in ARR as AI reshapes the legal market](https://betakit.com/clio-tops-500-million-usd-in-arr-as-ai-reshapes-the-legal-market/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian legal tech leader Clio has surpassed $500M USD in annual recurring revenue, demonstrating that AI-driven vertical SaaS companies built in Canada can achieve significant scale — a model relevant to fintech builders.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Feds back new group embedding graduate researchers with Canadian companies](https://betakit.com/feds-back-new-group-embedding-graduate-researchers-with-canadian-companies/)** — *BetaKit* — A new federally backed initiative will embed graduate researchers directly with Canadian companies, a talent pipeline model that Atlantic fintech startups and ecosystem builders could leverage to accelerate innovation.

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — This community resource cataloguing fintech failures and consumer harms serves as a timely reminder for Atlantic Canadian ecosystem builders about the importance of trust, transparency, and responsible product design.
